text: When no other evidence surfaced as is protocol, the synthetic aperture radar mission was concluded

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: and the investigation was handed off to the Homicide Bureau, the missing persons unit. But Monica's

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: by land and by drone when the official search and rescue mission was called off. Hikers have been

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: volunteer exhibitions kept going out every few days. Hope of rescue dimmed, but the goal was to at least

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: recover Monica's body. The organizers begged for volunteers to keep deploying until Monica's

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: birthday in December, six whole months after her disappearance. The weeks turned into months and

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: still no sign of Monica. She had vanished without a trace. That fateful June morning, Monica was well

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: equipped with a backpack, hiking boots, hiking pants, and plenty of water. It's still unclear if she had her

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: cell phone on her. Some theorized she could have been attacked by a mountain lion or bear, but no

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: dens were found in the area. No remains. The San Gabriels are mountain lion country. The terrain is

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: extremely rugged. There are many giant boulders and cave-like shelters that could obscure Monica from the

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: view of rescuers. Still, multiple civilian searchers who descended the ravine nearest to Monica's last

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: reported location, described the terrain as steep, but not steep enough to be fatal if someone fell.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Something about her disappearance feels off. If someone had wanted to kidnap Monica, it's conceivable

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: they could have intercepted her on the trail and led her to a car parked along a different section of

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: the highway. We may never know if her disappearance was just a tragic twist of fate, a crime of opportunity,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: or something much more nefarious. Beyond her invention of the Mondoloi, the heat-resistant

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: coating for interiors of rockets and satellites that allow for entry and re-entry into our atmosphere,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: most importantly, we want to honor Monica's life. The outpouring of prayer, support, and resources from

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: her family, friends, and colleagues demonstrates how deeply she is and will continue to be missed.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: The last entry on the volunteer Facebook group for Monica's search and rescue efforts was on

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: November 18, 2025. 150 days after her disappearance, the group was still planning a recovery expedition

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: that week. As disturbing as Monica's disappearance is, four days later, another woman disappeared.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: It's been nearly a month since anyone has seen Melissa Casillas. Her stepdaughter says a doorbell

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: camera showed Casillas walking a couple miles away with a backpack, and she was last seen walking on

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: a highway in Taos County about a month ago. It was June 26th. A family friend even saw her walking

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: along the highway. He turned around to see if she needed help, but then all he saw was a blue truck

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: driving by. No Casillas. Her family thinks she got into that truck. Her family says her personal

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: belongings are all still at home, including her phone, which had been factory reset.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: This time, an hour from Los Alamos National Laboratory in Ranchos de Taos. Melissa Casillas

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: is a 53-year-old administrative assistant at Los Alamos National Laboratory, one of the most

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: secretive research sites on Earth, and of course the original home to the Manhattan Project,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: where the first atomic bomb was developed under Oppenheimer.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: On the morning of June 26th, 2025, around 6.15am, Melissa dropped her husband, Mark, off at the

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: campus. He worked at the lab as a superintendent, and said he watched Melissa swipe her badge at the

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: gate. But instead of heading to work, Melissa drove an hour back home, telling her daughter

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Sierra. She'd forgotten her badge and was going to work from home instead. Sierra didn't think

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: anything of it, and left for her own job. Around 12.30, Melissa dropped off lunch for her daughter

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: at her work. Everything seemed normal, but around 1.30, Mark got a call from Melissa's boss. She never

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: showed up at work. He texted Sierra, who sent a text to her mom, and it was quickly marked as read.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: But by 2.30, her second text wouldn't deliver at all. Around 2.15, doorbell cameras caught Melissa

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: walking along State Road 518 in a turquoise shirt, blue jeans, and a maroon sweatshirt around her waist,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: heading in the direction of Carson National Forest, about three miles from her house. Back at the house,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: her daughter came home to a locked door and Melissa's car in the driveway. Inside, she found her mom's purse,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: wallet, keys, and both her work and personal phones. When she picked one of the phones up,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: she realized it had been factory reset. A check she was supposed to cash was sitting there too,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: next to a few dollar bills. Her daughter searched through her things and realized she might have

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: taken a toothbrush, a hair iron, and other personal items. A witness later reported that around the same

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: time Melissa was seen on surveillance footage, they saw a blue Dodge truck following her. They also

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: said she was walking like she was hurt or intoxicated. The family later disputed it,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: saying that the witness described a woman in a white shirt, not turquoise, which was what Melissa

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: was wearing. Either way, Melissa was gone. When her family, Sierra and Mark, compared notes,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: the stories didn't match up. Melissa told her daughter she came home because she forgot her badge,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: but Mark said he watched her swipe in with it. Either one of them was wrong,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: or Melissa wasn't telling the truth. After going through her things, Sierra realized that

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: her mother was under enormous pressure. On an interview with Dateline, she said that there

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: was a lot crumbling down on her that we didn't know about. Melissa was also feeling financial

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: stress. Her husband said that after their daughter Sierra was in a bad car accident,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: there was supposed to be a settlement, but it fell through. Her 2022 GoFundMe mentions that

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: the accident left her family with medical costs most could never fathom. As Sierra and her father

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: started piecing things together, they began suspecting Melissa might have left on her own.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: And when you look at the items she took, like a toothbrush and hair straightener,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: it tells us a few things. Abductees don't usually take their hair straighteners,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: and a suicidal person doesn't care about frizzy hair. This behavior describes someone who might be

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: expecting to go somewhere with running electricity, a mirror, and maybe even someone to see.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: The factory reset phones left at home are another clue. If she had left voluntarily,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: she might have wanted to wipe her message history and make herself harder to track down.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: The Los Alamos connection, of course, put Melissa on everyone's radar. And even though administrative

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: roles can overlap with classified material, we don't know what her position entailed.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: And when you add in the confusion about the badge, the factory reset phones, the personal items she

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: took, and the financial stress, this starts to look like someone who made a decision to disappear

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: on her own terms. Maybe she saw something she wasn't supposed to at the lab. And her phone and

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: hair appliance were a neat cover story for somebody who didn't want her to speak out. Whatever happened,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Melissa's still missing, and her family is still looking. Her parents have set up a GoFundMe,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: offering a $5,000 reward for any information that brings her home. But not every disappearance

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: near a sensitive facility is foul play. The evidence looks like it points somewhere more

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: tragic and personal than a sinister conspiracy. We're including her because she deserves to be

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: found, but we're not including her as evidence of something darker. For that, we have to look 700 miles

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: west at what was happening under the stars just outside Los Angeles.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: A man shot and killed three days ago at his home in the Antelope Valley community of Lano has been

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: identified as Carl Grillmayer. He was a Caltech scientist. Colleagues say the 67-year-old made

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: groundbreaking discoveries in astronomy and will be greatly missed.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: On February 16, 2026, about 30 miles northeast of Waterman Mountain, where Monica Reza disappeared,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: another colleague from Caltech and JPL would meet an equally tragic and suspicious end—the great

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: astronomer Carl Grillmayer. In the small, unincorporated community of Lano, tucked away

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: near the Los Angeles and San Bernardino County line, a world-class astronomer had built his own

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: observatory on a remote stretch of land. He chose to set up shop in the secluded Antelope Valley,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: 20 miles east of Palmdale, precisely because of how thinly populated it was. Carl Grillmayer wanted

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: the darkest night skies possible, with the least amount of light pollution. Carl Johan Grillmayer,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: born in Calgary, Alberta in 1959, dedicated his life to studying galactic astronomy in distant planets.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: His work focused on mapping the structure of the Milky Way, identifying stellar streams,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: remnants of smaller galaxies, or clusters torn apart by gravitational forces. These incredibly

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: faint, stretched out ribbons of stars, drift through our Milky Way, and tell the story of what was left

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: behind. Modern science sometimes suffers from hyper-specialization, but Carl Grillmayer did not.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Within astrophysics, he was a renaissance man, a polymath, with research interests at every scale.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: From the research of our own solar system, to giant galaxy clusters, to the search for extraterrestrial

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: life. Grillmayer discovered the Lethe Stream, a vast river of stars yanked into the Milky Way from

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: nearby globular clusters. Using the Sloan Digital Survey, or SDSS, Grillmayer tracked subtle disturbances

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: in the paths of these stellar streams, leading to key insights into dark matter, a mysterious missing

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: mass, which forms a cosmic glue that holds galaxies and their clusters together. Stars, galaxies,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: planets, and matter as we know it are only 5% of the observable universe. Think of them as the foam

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: on an incoming wave crashing on the beach. Dark matter forms much of the rest of the ocean.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: A vast halo of dark matter envelops the Milky Way. Its immense gravity would disturb the path of incoming

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: stellar streams by tugging on them and changing their paths. And by tracking these changes,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Grillmayer was able to make profound insights into one of the universe's biggest mysteries.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: What is most of it made out of? Through his comprehensive study of stellar streams,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Grillmayer helped reshape our understanding of how galaxies evolve. And his work with exoplanets was

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: arguably even more profound. Using the Spitzer Space Telescope, Grillmayer did pioneering work

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: studying the atmospheres of exoplanets, breaking down light passing through distant worlds to search

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: for molecular fingerprints that tell us what they're made of. In 2007, Grillmayer was part of the team

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: that discovered the first ever instance of water vapor in an exoplanet's atmosphere. Today, the techniques

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: he pioneered are proving pivotal in the scientific search for alien life, as the same capabilities

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: can now probe the atmospheres of habitable planets for biosignatures, footprints of living ecology

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: altering the planet's chemical balance. His colleague at Caltech, Sergio Faiardo Acosta,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: described his approach to exoplanets and galactic structures as truly detective work.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Carl Grillmayer was an extremely renowned scientist, and I would say he still is because

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: his legacy will keep on. And it becomes the consensus reality in conventional astronomy that

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: we're not alone will owe a lot of that paradigm-shattering insight to Dr. Grillmayer's groundwork.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: But Grillmayer also studied celestial bodies much closer to home. He worked closely with NEOWISE,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: an instrument which serves as planetary defense. You heard me right.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: NEOWISE could be the first line of defense against humans going the way of the dinosaurs.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: So Grillmayer had as front line of view as you can get when it came to the potential for Earth's

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: extinction. He's published extensively, and his research has earned him a number of accolades,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: including NASA's Exceptional Scientific Achievement Medal in 2011. Recently, Grillmayer had begun work on

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: a new project, testing new instrumentation at Caltech's Palomar Observatory to monitor for

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: meteor impacts on the Moon's surface during an upcoming lunar eclipse. Another colleague,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: collaborator, and lead scientist, Joe Maciero, said,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: It is a really exciting project, and I know Carl was looking forward to seeing what we could learn

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: about the near-space environment from that. It made perfect sense that someone so passionate

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: about the night skies would build a home outfitted with his own observatory. And what makes no sense

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: is why a neighbor would seemingly stalk him on his property in December and return to kill him in

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: February. On December 20th, 2025, according to sheriff officials in court records, Carl Grillmayer

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: called the police to report someone trespassing on his sprawling lot in Llano. Deputies were dispatched,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: and when they arrived, they found 29-year-old Llano resident Freddy Snyder wandering the rugged

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: landscape nearby, carrying a loaded unregistered rifle. Snyder claimed he was just headed to the post

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: office and carried the weapon for self-defense against wild animals. But the LA Times uncovered

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Snyder's property records, indicating the post office was in the opposite direction of his and

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Grillmayer's home. The sheriffs arrested Snyder on a felony weapons charge and booked him at the

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Palmdale Station Jail, where he was accused of attempting to escape before his court appearance.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: When Snyder showed up at court, the judge told him to complete a gun safety course,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: citing his lack of past criminal record and an unnecessary prosecution's law as the reason for

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: his leniency. Snyder was released from jail on his own recognizance. Things calmed in the new year,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: at least temporarily, until another 911 call came in on February 16th. At 6.10am, deputies responded to

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: a 911 call for assault with a deadly weapon. Carl Grillmayer had been shot on his porch. Paramedics

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: pronounced him dead at the scene. While the deputies responded to the 911 call at the Grillmayer residence,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: another call came in. A carjacking had occurred just down the road. Freddy Snyder was arrested for

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: the carjacking and was subsequently linked to Grillmayer's shooting. Snyder, the man who was

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: released from custody for lacking a criminal record, was now charged with several felonies, including

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: burglary, carjacking, and murder. This time, his bail was set at just above $3 million. Investigators have

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: yet to uncover a motive, and they've found no evidence that Snyder and Grillmayer were acquainted.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: No one has an explanation for why this man suddenly went on a crime spree that began and ended with Carl

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Grillmayer. And Freddy Snyder's arraignment was postponed from March 26th to April 29th, so it may take

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: many months to uncover just what happened here. Could Grillmayer have stumbled upon something he

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: wasn't supposed to? Was Snyder being controlled by darker forces? Was he just a patsy or an extension

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: of something deeper? Recently, Dr. Grillmayer had been working on the revolutionary Vera Rubin Observatory,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: an observatory which saw its first light mere months before his untimely death. The Vera Rubin Observatory is

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: one of the largest scale surveys of the sky ever undertaken. It produces over a thousand images

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: every night, covering the entire southern hemisphere from horizon to horizon, and it promises to

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: revolutionize the search for interstellar objects. Currently, we've only ever detected three objects

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: confirmed to be entering the solar system from beyond. But astronomers estimate Vera Rubin will discover

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: 50 by the time its run is over. It will show us our own solar system in unprecedented detail.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: On its first act of night, it revealed over 2,000 previously undiscovered asteroids. Here's where

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: things take an interesting, albeit very speculative, turn. In 2017, we discovered Oumuamua, the first ever

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: interstellar object to visit our solar system. It had some very strange properties, especially the way it

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: accelerated without any visible cometary tail, leading past American alchemy guest and Harvard

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: astrophysicist Avi Loeb to speculate that it may be a spacecraft or alien artifact. It was given the name

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Oumuamua because it means in the Hawaiian language, a scout, a messenger from far away. All the proposals

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: that were put on the table to explain the anomalies of Oumuamua invoked a rock of a type that we've never seen

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: before. With Rubin now online, we're poised to discover many more Oumuamua's, which is why Vera

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Rubin is now motivating mainstream SETI researchers to start scanning our solar system for more anomalies

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: which could be alien spacecraft or probes. If our solar system is in fact filled in extraterrestrial

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: spacecrafts and artifacts from visiting civilizations, Rubin is perfectly poised to pick them up. But there's also

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: a big limitation. All of Rubin's imagery has to be approved by the Pentagon. This is not a conspiracy

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: theory. It's a publicly known fact as reported in this article from the Atlantic. Every image gets reviewed

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: by the intelligence agencies before the scientific community is allowed to see it. As the Atlantic article

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: reports, a government agency would chip in five million dollars for the construction of a dedicated network

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: for moving sensitive data. Each time the telescope were to take one of its 30-second tile images of

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: the sky, the file would be immediately encrypted without anyone looking at it first and then sent

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: to a secure facility in California. Next, an automated system would compare the image with previous images

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: of the same tile. It would cut out small postage stamp pictures of any new object it finds,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: be they asteroids, exploding stars, or spy satellites. It would filter out the postage

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: stamps that might depict secret US assets, and one minute later, send all the rest together with

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: their coordinates to an alert service available to astronomers worldwide. This level of intense

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: intelligence community oversight over what you might expect to be a conventional astronomical tool

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: is not unprecedented. A much smaller all-sky survey called PANSTARS, the same one which discovered

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Oumuamua, underwent massive censorship from the Air Force, with swaths of imagery redacted or blacked out.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Astronomers complained that these redactions often got in the way of their work. Now what if you were to

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: spot a UFO on one of these things? Of course the publicly stated reason for this has nothing to do with UFOs,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: and everything to do with classified spy satellites and military space assets. Most of the time this

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: is probably true, but it does have other implications. Past American alchemist Dr. Beatrice Villareal

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: discovered a possibly vast population of unknown objects orbiting the Earth. She found tens of

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: thousands of transients, light reflecting mirror-like objects orbiting the Earth. And she found them on

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: astronomical plates from 1949 to 1957, before Sputnik or any American satellites were up in space. She

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: also found them on astronomical plates from the Palomar Observatory, the same place associated with Carl

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Grillmair. Villareal is currently working on replicating her results. And if I had to guess,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: I think we're going to find some interesting corroboration for them.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: I've been working with transients for a while. I think a lot of people know about this transient

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: work. We have been looking for like multiple transients and images. Sometimes you can see

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: multiple of them appearing and vanishing within half an hour. So if there truly is a population of UFOs

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: surrounding the Earth, the Rubin telescope, Grillmair's pet project at the end of his life,

text: brings us to another shocking and senseless act of violence that took out another of the world's

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: preeminent scientific minds, this time an expert on nuclear fusion. Nuno Felipe Gómez Lurero was born

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: in 1977 in Visu, a city in central Portugal. Even as a little boy, he always knew he wanted to be a

text: scientist. In a 2018 MIT profile, Nuno recalled how everyone else wanted to be a policeman or a fireman.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: He followed that passion to Lisbon, where he received his undergraduate and master's degree

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: at the Instituto Superior Tecnico in Lisbon. Nuno then went on to attend Imperial College in London,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: earning a doctorate in physics in 2005, with a dissertation on tearing modes in plasma.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: After graduating, Nuno arrived in the United States to join Princeton University as a post-doctoral

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: researcher at the Plasma Physics Lab in 2005. From 2007 to 2016, Nuno worked in a laboratory for the UK

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Atomic Energy Authority and as a researcher at the Plasma and Nuclear Fusion Institute in Portugal.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: In 2016, Lurero returned to the United States, joining the faculty at MIT as a professor and fusion

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: scientist. I'm Nuno Lurero. I'm a professor at MIT. My main appointment is in nuclear science and

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: engineering. He flourished at MIT and by 2022 became deputy director of MIT's largest lab, the Plasma Science

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: and Fusion Center, which is an umbrella research center at MIT for all the plasma and fusion-related

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: activities that we do on campus.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: And in January 2025, President Joe Biden presented him with the Presidential Early Career Award,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: the highest U.S. government honor for young scientists.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: To say Nuno was a renowned physicist would be a massive understatement.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: He was a leading expert in plasma physics. The work Nuno was conducting at MIT was attempting to

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: solve the hardest problems in nuclear fusion.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Problems that would unlock our capabilities in clean fusion power,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: potentially solving the world's energy crisis forever.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: For those unaware, nuclear fusion would be the ultimate clean energy dream,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: bringing the sun to the earth and powering our whole society.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: No fossil fuel pollution or Chernobyl-style meltdown necessary.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: Wielding fusion power would dramatically reduce the human need for fossil fuels.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: And with that, we might be able to avoid a lot of the geopolitical escapades we're seeing happening today.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: But there's one great challenge. Plasma is a chaotic soup of charged ionized gas,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: which behaves in wildly unpredictable ways. To contain it, and to force the atoms to fuse,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: demands a special magnetic field, one which binds the plasma up into just the right geometry,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: and under just enough pressure to force the atoms to merge, despite the immense repulsive force

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: between them. There are many challenges to nuclear fusion, but perhaps the greatest

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: is the problem of the containment field. It is no simple task, but it's where Nuno Lurero excelled.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: His research was especially focused on one of the greatest challenges in plasma containment fields,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: magnetic reconnection. Consider the loops of plasma we see on the sun. Plasma gets trapped

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: in arcs of opposite magnetic force. It usually gets trapped in tubes of electromagnetic energy,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: hot particles rising out of the sun's surface and falling back in. Sometimes, like a rubber band

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: that's been stretched too much, it snaps. The field lines break. All the energy stored in the field

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: bursts out as the particles heat up and accelerate out. Then the field reconnects. This process causes

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: solar flares and coronal mass ejections, the kind of events that could one day fry our entire grid

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: with an electromagnetic pulse, or EMP. Nuno was a real blue sky researcher,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: studying the phenomenon of magnetic reconnection itself.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: But his work had extremely practical applications, because just as reconnection causes flares from the sun,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: it's also a major factor in our attempts to bring the sun's power down to Earth, and do so in the form of

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: real nuclear fusion. Giant donut-shaped tokamak reactors use powerful magnets to contain superheated plasma,

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: and magnetic reconnection is one of the greatest obstacles to sustained nuclear fusion. Just like plasma

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: ejections from the sun, often with fusion, a leak gets created, leading to rapid cooling and pressure loss.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: This effectively stops the fusion process dead in its tracks. Nuno Lurero was one of the world's

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: experts in understanding the intricacies of exactly how and why these magnetic reconnection leaks happen.

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: We don't know if Nuno came close to solving the problem of magnetic field line breaks. But we do know

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: he was probably as close as anybody. And at 47 years old, it's safe to say he had many impressive

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
text: decades of discovery and invention ahead of him. Except on December 15th, Dr. Nuno Lurero was gunned down

transcript_title: Why Americas Top Scientists Are Going M
